subject: Mathematics
activityTitle: Fig. 1   Fig. 2 4. Created crease is a tangent to the circle from the point P. Mark the point of
objective:
To find the height of a building using a
clinometer.
Clinometer (a stand fitted with a
square plate which is fitted with a
movable 0º–360º protractor and a
straw), a measuring tape 50 m long,
table or stool.
procedure:
1. Place a table on the ground of a school.
2. Place a clinometer (a stand fitted with 0º–360º protractor and a straw whose
central line coincides with 0º–360º line) on the table.
3. Now face it towards the building of the school.
4. Peep out through the straw to the top of the school building and note the
angle ( θ ) through which the protractor turns   from 0º–360º line.
observation:

1. Length of tangent QP = ...............
2. Length of tangent RP = ...............
So, length of tangent QP = length of tangent .................
discussion:
This result is useful in solving problems in geometry and mensuration.
